# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Istio IngressGateway configuration that routes HTTP traffic to services. Configure Gateway for port 80 without TLS for simplicity. Use HTTP for all ingress traffic since we have a load balancer handling TLS termination upstream. This is for our Istio service mesh ingress.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3  # version of the API that you're using (as per https://github.com/istio/community/blob/master/enhancements/Istio_Release_Notes-CN.md)
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: istio-ingressgateway   # unique identifier for this gateway in the traffic management system, e.g., Istiod or Pilot if you're using Kubernetes service mesh controller like kube-proxy (which we will not use here)
spec:
  selector:    # select which services can access your sidecar by matching metadata labels and annotations on Gateway/DestinationRule objects. See the following example for how to do it with a destination rule annotation, or using HTTP headers if you prefer simpler routing rules (like header match). In this case we use all endpoints here
    istio: ingressgateway   # label that identifies your gateway in traffic management system e.g., Istiod / Pilot kube-proxy will identify the Gateways based on their annotations or labels set by user (like sidecar injection)  like 'istio'=ingressgateway
